sessions.lever: pin removal + reorder-rule strip; swept 6 decl/init-order variants (bitpos-first decl, bitpos-before-sum init, sum-last, bitpos-last). All >=9.

8 more pure-C variants from candidate.c (floor 9) — shift-tmp split (1<<bitpos to s32 sh), plain `register` storage-class hint (no asm pin), shift-tmp+register combined, p=&D_800A32D0 materialized FIRST in init, for-loop with bitpos in for-clause, ANSI decl+init bitpos-first, arg1<<=4 hoisted to init head, arg1<<=4 deferred past zero-inits. Plain `register` hint scored 9 (no effect); ALL others scored 11-17 (worse). Confirms the wall is NOT source-orderable. Attempted permuter import on candidate.c — import.py warned about a sibling func_8004A348 inline-asm body and the subsequent compile.sh hit a maspsx 'too many values to unpack' exception; permuter needs an import-pipeline patch BEFORE it can drive this function.

variants_tried.form: plain `register` (no asm pin) on sum+bitpos, bitpos decl first
rewrite do/while as for(bitpos=0,i=0; i<2; bitpos+=0x10,p++,i++)
arg1<<=4 hoisted to first init line

reviewer.evidence: candidate.c is plain pure C: removes both register asm pins; the regfix reorder rule is NOT in candidate (it lives in regfix.txt). Confirmed NOT byte-matching (score 9), so not applied to src/. No coercions of any kind.

remaining_gap.summary: TWO coupled diffs (9 insns total via register-name propagation): (1) li t6,1 (hoisted loop-invariant constant 1) prologue position — target schedules it right after `sll a1,a1,0x4`; our build emits it after the `lui/addiu t4` pair for p=&D_800A32D0. (2) sum/bitpos register swap — target sum=$t2($10), bitpos=$t1($9); GCC gives us the reverse. The sum/bitpos swap propagates through every sllv/addu/move that touches them (~7 insns); the li-hoist is the remaining 2.
remaining_gap.cheat_at_head: 2x register asm("$10") (sum), asm("$9") (bitpos) pins + the reorder rule
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: The reorder rule fixes diff (1) (li hoist position); the two pins fix diff (2) (sum/bitpos swap). Both are forbidden. No pure-C lever found that reaches either; they appear coupled (the scheduler's li placement and the allocator's t1/t2 choice).

next_hypotheses: decomp-permuter from candidate.c (floor 9): this is exactly the scheduling+RA coupling the permuter's randomization targets. Setup is non-trivial (project maspsx/fix_lwl pipeline) — but it is the documented next step when manual decl/init-order levers plateau. NB project memory permuter-closability-evaluated rates the remaining cheat-carrying functions as largely NOT permuter-closable; treat as a genuine attempt, not a guarantee.

li-hoist (diff 1): investigate whether changing how p=&D_800A32D0 is materialized (e.g. compute its address via an index off a base, or defer it) lets the scheduler float li t6,1 up to right after the sll. The lui/addiu t4 pair currently occupies the slot target gives to li.

sum/bitpos swap (diff 2): the swap is in the $t1/$t2 ($9/$10) band, both vars live across the whole loop. sum is the return value (move v0,t2). Try whether returning via an explicit fresh var, or restructuring the accumulation, flips the allocator's t1<->t2 tie. Decl/init order alone does NOT (measured).
Instrumented-cc1 SCHED + PRIO dumps (BB2_SCHED_DEBUG / BB2_PRIO_DEBUG per register-alloc-deep-dive) to see WHY sched places li late and WHY the allocator ties sum to the lower reg.
